query: honestly... I'm not an expert on graph structures or really any of this intelligence...   Here's what I know.  We have a knowledge intelligence engine that can track exactly how knowledge evolves.  We support semantic query, and our hope is to enable the ability to detect information relevant to agents/systems they don't even know they want... simply becaues we know more about what they are doing than any individual agent or system does.  WE HAVE ALL OF THEIR CONTEXT.  Our goal, through graph, heuristics, ML, and LM's is to determine what they want and deliver it.  So we've got a plan, with may individual delivery items... and our job is to ensure the way in which we put these together can do just that.  The NLI use case we're talking about is important as 1 decomposed capability for contradictions/similarities... but also surfacing these to the requester also has a decision component to it (this is the GGUF aspect.. at least in my uneducated head).   but... if we can delivery our objectives w/out that complexity/weight... thats good too.  Thoughts?
